rulings.Case Title: 530 JACKSON STREET, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. ACCEPTANCE INDEMNITY INSURANCE COMPANY, A et al

Continued to October 13, 2009 in Department 302 at 9:30 am. Plaintiff to provide courtesy copies of its opposition papers to Department 302 per Local Rule 2.6 with a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: SEAN C MCKEAN et al VS. STEPHEN E LAWRENCE et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, September 22, 2009, line 3, DEFENDANT SOPHIE GASPARATOS, and, STEPHEN LAWRENCE DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Demurrer overruled in part and sustained in part. Demurrer overruled as to first, sixth, and eighth as to plaintiff McIlvenna, ninth and Twelfth causes of action. Demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend as to the seventh cause of action: Breach of warranty of habitability must be based on physical defects not misconduct. However, the complaint, on its face, not time-barred or barred by settlement agreement. Demurrer sustained without leave to amend as to eighth cause of action regarding plaintiff McKean who has not vacted premises. (See Cunningham vs. Universal Underwriters (2002) 98 Cal.App.4th 1141, 1152.) Amended complaint will indicate eighth cause of action pertains to plaintiff McIlvenna only. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: ZENDA SU VS. HERMAN CHEN et al*****CASE TRANSFERRED TO TEHAMA COUNTY*****
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice And Motion To Change Venue
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, September 22, 2009, line 7, DEFENDANT OLIVE NINETY-NINE CORPORATION Motion To Change Venue. Motion for change of venue granted. Case is transferred to Tehama County, the corporation's principal place of business. (CCP section 395.5.) Request for attorney fees denied. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT S. VAILE VS. DELOITTE & TOUCHE, LLP
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Robert S. Vaile'S Order To Show Cause Re Preliminary Injunction
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, September 22, 2009, line 8, Plaintiff Robert S. Vaile's Order to Show Cause regarding preliminary injunction. Motion for preliminary injunction granted. The Court finds that Willick's collection efforts are improper and do not comply with Code of Civil Procedure section 1710.15 et seq. Plaintiff likely to prevail due to lack of a properly domesticated order. Plaintiff will suffer irreparable harm without the injunction due to lack of funds to meet plaintiff's nondiscretionary obligations. Plaintiff to post a bond in the amount of $10,000.00 on or before September 29, 2009. (CCP section 529.) If he seeks to contest jurisdiction, Willick to bring a proper motion to quash. Court denies plaintiff's request for sanctions. No compliance with safe harbor provision of CCP section 128.7 and a reply brief is not the proper vehicle to request sanctions. =(302/CWW)
